data.content: Now I'll create the `.template.config/template.json` to turn this into a reusable template.
data.reasoningText:
Now I need to create a .NET template from this project. To do this, I need to:

1. Create a `.template.config` directory inside the project folder
2. Create a `template.json` file with the template configuration
3. The template should use `sourceName` to allow the project name to be replaced when instantiated

Key things:
- The template identity and shortName need to be set
- `sourceName` should be "MyTests" so it gets replaced with the new project name
- The namespace in UnitTest1.cs uses "MyTests" so it will be replaced automatically
- The .csproj file name also uses "MyTests"

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-7cb06329532c44bda5a4b4fdcd1bf8b7/test-output/MyTests/.template.config/template.json b/tmp/sv-7cb06329532c44bda5a4b4fdcd1bf8b7/test-output/MyTests/.template.config/template.json
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-7cb06329532c44bda5a4b4fdcd1bf8b7/test-output/MyTests/.template.config/template.json
@@ -1,0 +1,15 @@
+{
+  "$schema": "http://json.schemastore.org/template",
+  "author": "Team",
+  "classifications": ["Test", "xUnit"],
+  "identity": "TeamTestProject",
+  "name": "Team Test Project",
+  "shortName": "team-test",
+  "tags": {
+    "language": "C#",
+    "type": "project"
+  },
+  "sourceName": "MyTests",
+  "preferNameDirectory": true
+}
+

The template "Team Test Project" was created successfully.

---csproj---
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net9.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.9.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2">
      <IncludeAssets>runtime; build; native; contentfiles; analyzers; buildtransitive</IncludeAssets>
      <PrivateAssets>all</PrivateAssets>
    </PackageReference>
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
---cs---
using Xunit;

namespace SampleTests;
public class UnitTest1
{
    [Fact]
    public void Test1() { }
}
<exited with exit code 0>

data.content: Template works perfectly. Let me clean up the test project:

Done! I created `.template.config/template.json` in your project. Here's what it does:

- **Template name**: "Team Test Project"
- **Short name**: `team-test` (used on the CLI)
- **Source name replacement**: `MyTests` → automatically replaced with the new project name (in filenames, namespaces, etc.)

Your team can use it like this:

```bash
dotnet new install ./test-output/MyTests   # install once
dotnet new team-test -n MyNewTests         # create new projects
```
